description: SQLite database file format. SQLite is a self-contained, serverless, zero-configuration, transactional SQL database engine. The file begins with a 100-byte header containing the magic string, page size, format versions, and database configuration parameters.
author: WPFHexaEditor Team

functions.parseSQLiteHeader: Parse SQLite database header and extract all configuration parameters from the 100-byte header
functions.formatFileSize(var:databaseSizeBytes): Format total database size as a human-readable string
functions.computeFromVariables(pageSize * database: Calculate total database size in bytes from page size and page count
functions.computeFromVariables(fileFormatWriteVers: Determine if the database uses Write-Ahead Logging mode

blocks.name: SQLite Header String
blocks.description: Magic string 'SQLite format 3\000' (16 bytes including null terminator) identifying a valid SQLite database file

blocks.name: Page Size
blocks.description: Database page size in bytes. Must be a power of 2 between 512 and 65536. A value of 1 represents 65536 bytes.

blocks.name: File Format Write Version
blocks.description: File format write version. Determines the journal mode used for write transactions.

blocks.name: File Format Read Version
blocks.description: File format read version. Must be 1 or 2. A reader must understand this version to read the database.

blocks.name: Reserved Space per Page
blocks.description: Bytes of unused space reserved at the end of each page. Usually 0. Used by extensions like SQLite Encryption Extension (SEE).

blocks.name: Max Embedded Payload Fraction
blocks.description: Maximum embedded payload fraction. Must be 64. Determines maximum payload stored on internal b-tree pages.

blocks.name: Min Embedded Payload Fraction
blocks.description: Minimum embedded payload fraction. Must be 32. Minimum payload stored on internal b-tree pages before overflow.

blocks.name: Leaf Payload Fraction
blocks.description: Leaf payload fraction. Must be 32. Minimum payload stored on leaf b-tree pages before overflow.

blocks.name: File Change Counter
blocks.description: Incremented each time the database file is unlocked after being modified. Used to detect if the file has changed.

blocks.name: Database Size in Pages
blocks.description: Total number of pages in the database file. If 0, the value is computed from the actual file size.

blocks.name: First Freelist Trunk Page
blocks.description: Page number of the first freelist trunk page. 0 if the freelist is empty.

blocks.name: Total Freelist Pages
blocks.description: Total number of pages on the freelist. These are unused pages available for reuse.

blocks.name: Schema Cookie
blocks.description: Incremented each time the database schema changes. Used by prepared statements to detect schema changes.

blocks.name: Schema Format Number
blocks.description: Schema format number. Determines the schema syntax and capabilities supported by this database.

valueMap.2: Format 2 (SQLite 3.1.3+ - adds CAST and compound SELECT)
valueMap.3: Format 3 (SQLite 3.1.4+ - adds IF NOT EXISTS, RENAME TABLE)
valueMap.4: Format 4 (SQLite 3.3.0+ - adds DESC index, COLLATE in CREATE TABLE)

blocks.name: Default Page Cache Size
blocks.description: Suggested page cache size in pages for the default pager. Set by 'PRAGMA default_cache_size'.

blocks.name: Largest Root B-Tree Page
blocks.description: Page number of the largest root b-tree page in auto-vacuum or incremental-vacuum mode. 0 otherwise.

blocks.name: Text Encoding
blocks.description: Text encoding used for all text strings in the database. Set once when the database is created.

blocks.name: User Version
blocks.description: User-defined version number. Set and read via 'PRAGMA user_version'. Not used by SQLite internally.

blocks.name: Incremental Vacuum Mode
blocks.description: Non-zero if incremental vacuum mode is enabled. Set by 'PRAGMA incremental_vacuum'. 0 means auto-vacuum or none.

blocks.name: Application ID
blocks.description: Application-defined integer set via 'PRAGMA application_id'. Used to identify the database file format for applications (e.g., for file type detection).

blocks.name: SQLite Version Number
blocks.description: SQLite library version that most recently modified the database. Encoded as MAJOR*1000000 + MINOR*1000 + RELEASE (e.g., 3039004 = 3.39.4).

blocks.name: Database Size (bytes)
blocks.description: Calculate the total database file size in bytes by multiplying page size by total number of pages

blocks.name: WAL Mode Flag
blocks.description: Determine if the database is configured for Write-Ahead Logging (WAL) mode based on the write version byte

blocks.name: WAL Mode Detection
blocks.trueLabel: Database uses WAL (Write-Ahead Logging) mode for concurrent reads/writes
blocks.falseLabel: Database uses legacy rollback journal mode
blocks.description: Checks the file format write version to determine if WAL journaling is enabled (version 2) or legacy rollback journal (version 1)

blocks.name: Incremental Vacuum Check
blocks.falseLabel: Incremental vacuum mode is disabled (auto-vacuum or none)
blocks.description: Checks whether incremental vacuum mode is active, which allows the application to control when freed pages are returned to the filesystem

blocks.name: Page Size
blocks.description: Database page size in bytes (power of 2, typically 4096)

blocks.name: Database Size (pages)
blocks.description: Total number of pages in the database file

blocks.name: Text Encoding
blocks.description: Text encoding used for all strings: 1=UTF-8, 2=UTF-16le, 3=UTF-16be

blocks.name: SQLite Version
blocks.description: SQLite library version that last modified the database (encoded as MAJOR*1000000+MINOR*1000+RELEASE)

blocks.name: Schema Version
blocks.description: Schema format number (1-4) indicating supported SQL features

blocks.name: Total Freelist Pages
blocks.description: Number of unused pages on the freelist available for reuse

TechnicalDetails.Endianness: big-endian (all multi-byte values in the header)
